channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: China and North Korea strengthen diplomatic ties ahead of treaty anniversary
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping met North Korean Premier Pak Thae Song in Beijing to mark the 65th anniversary of their historic friendship treaty. The meeting, part of Pak’s three-day visit, highlights ...

item.title: Former Okada Rider Arrested for Smuggling 100 Meth Wraps at Lagos Airport
item.description: A former okada rider, Onyechere Daniel Chinedu, was arrested by the NDLEA after attempting to smuggle 100 wraps of methamphetamine into Nigeria. The suspect swallowed the drugs in Uganda but could onl...

item.title: Ex-TMC MLA's son arrested for post-election violence and lottery ticket theft
item.description: The West Bengal police arrested Tirthankar Ghosh, son of former TMC MLA Nirmal Ghosh, in connection with a 2021 post-election violence case and a 2024 incident where he allegedly snatched a prize-winn...

item.title: Mastodon release fifth studio album 'Marrow Deep' with Josh Homme collaboration
item.description: Mastodon have announced their highly anticipated fifth studio album, 'Marrow Deep', marking their first full-length work in five years and their debut without late guitarist Brent Hinds. The album, se...

item.title: Springboks coach Rassie Erasmus prepares for intense rivalry against reinvigorated All Blacks under Dave Rennie
item.description: Springboks coach Rassie Erasmus has expressed concerns about the physicality of the All Blacks under new head coach Dave Rennie, acknowledging the team's rejuvenated form ahead of the Rugby's Greatest...

item.title: South African man arrested for murdering family in UK after fleeing country
item.description: A South African man suspected of murdering his wife and two children in the UK has been arrested and extradited to face justice. The suspect fled to Zimbabwe before entering South Africa, where author...

item.title: US-Iran Clash Over Hormuz Strait Escalates, Oil Prices Rise
item.description: The conflict between the United States and Iran has intensified with renewed missile and drone attacks over the Strait of Hormuz, a critical shipping route for global oil. After Iran declared the stra...

item.title: Key Senate leaders' absence and sudden death disrupt congressional legislative agenda
item.description: The sudden passing of Senator Lindsey Graham and the prolonged absence of Senator Mitch McConnell are creating significant challenges for the U.S. Congress as it resumes sessions after its July 4 holi...

item.title: Government plans to involve skilled artisans in vocational training to address teacher shortage
item.description: The Tanzanian government is addressing a persistent shortage of vocational education teachers by planning to involve experienced mechanics and workshop artisans in providing practical training to stud...

item.title: Australian Government Addresses AI Regulation and Workplace Impact
item.description: The article discusses the Australian government's cautious approach to regulating artificial intelligence as it navigates the challenges posed by rapid technological advancement. With concerns about j...

item.title: Northern Aqueduct Temporary Shutdown for Essential Repairs Affects Multiple Areas
item.description: The Northern Aqueduct, which supplies water to Durban Heights Water Treatment Works, will undergo a planned temporary shutdown from 1am on Tuesday, July 14, to 1am on Wednesday, July 15, to facilitate...

item.title: Teodoro prioritizes defense over 2028 election focus
item.description: Gilberto Teodoro Jr., son of former Philippine President Gloria Macapagal-Arroyo, emphasized that his focus remains on national defense initiatives rather than the upcoming 2028 presidential elections...

item.title: Naomie Harris and Peter Legler's long-term relationship profile
item.description: British actress Naomie Harris, known for her role as Winnie Mandela in 'Mandela: Long Walk to Freedom,' has been in a decade-long relationship with Peter Legler, a private British investor. Legler, fr...

item.title: Legal Challenge Against Registrar's Decision in Al-Qadir Trust Case
item.description: Former Prime Minister Imran Khan and his wife Bushra Bibi have filed a legal challenge against the Supreme Court registrar's decision to reject their petitions seeking suspension of sentences in the A...

item.title: Former Taraba Governor's Official Testifies to Misuse of State Funds
item.description: An official testified in court that N1.8 billion belonging to Gassol Local Government in Taraba State was illegally transferred into his company's account during former Governor Darius Ishaku's admini...

item.title: PM Shehbaz Offers Condolences to Qatar After Former Emir Sheikh Hamad's Death, Declares National Mourning Day
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif arrived in Doha for a day-long visit and met Qatar’s emir Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani to offer condolences on the death of former emir Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Tha...

item.title: Analyst warns Trump's Iran conflict risks political downfall amid economic fallout
item.description: Political analyst Trita Parsi argues that President Donald Trump is nearing his 'economic and political pain threshold' due to the escalating U.S.-Iran conflict, which could jeopardize his re-election...

item.title: UK Bans Support for Iran's IRGC and Other Groups Amid National Security Threats
item.description: Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood has designated Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) as a threat to national security, enabling the banning of support for the group. The IRGC, linked to attac...

item.title: Australia to introduce digital passenger cards at all international airports
item.description: News Corp Australia has issued a notice regarding the implementation of digital passenger cards at all Australian international airports. The article explains that the rollout is part of a broader ini...

item.title: Businessman Arrested Over Arson Attacks Causing R800,000 Damage in Polokwane
item.description: A businessman in Polokwane, South Africa, has been arrested over two arson incidents that caused approximately R800,000 in damage. The suspect, identified through police investigations, was apprehende...
